result:
Fix verified: long strings now have distinct `%p` identities, short strings still share. No temp instrumentation to remove. I have not re-run the full strings.lua test; the mega-loop will re-test.

## Summary

**Root cause:** `LuaState::intern_str()` in `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1182` was globally interning *every* byte sequence into `interned_lt`, including long strings. Lua 5.4 only interns short strings (≤ `LUAI_MAXSHORTLEN` = 40 bytes); long strings must each get a fresh object identity so that `string.format("%p", s1) ~= string.format("%p", s2)` holds for two distinct long strings (strings.lua line 194: `local s1 = string.rep("a", 300); local s2 = string.rep("a", 300); assert(... ~= ...)`).

**Fix:** Gated the global intern path behind `bytes.len() <= MAX_SHORT_LEN`; long strings now return a fresh `GcRef::new(LuaString::from_bytes(...))` per call. Exposed `MAX_SHORT_LEN` as `pub(crate)` in `crates/lua-vm/src/string.rs`. Doc comment updated to reflect correct semantics.

**New state:** My focused repro (`string.rep("a", 300)` twice with `%p`) now shows distinct addresses (`0x...3190` vs `0x...3220`) where it previously shared. Build is green (`cargo build -p lua-cli`). The mega-loop will re-test strings.lua end-to-end.

tool_input.command:
cat > /tmp/test_q.lua << 'EOF'
local x = "\0\1\0023\5\0009"
local q = string.format('%q', x)
print('q=', q)
print('xlen=', #x)
print('rlen=', #load(string.format('return %q', x))())
for i=1,#x do io.write(string.byte(x,i),' ') end print()
local r = load(string.format('return %q', x))()
for i=1,#r do io.write(string.byte(r,i),' ') end print()
EOF
target/debug/lua-rs /tmp/test_q.lua 2>&1 | tail -20
git stash && cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>&1 | grep -E "^error" | head -5; git stash pop
